summary: Add 'todo sync' command that pushes ticket state to a Hermes Kanban board over HTTP
description:
Add `todo sync` that POSTs the current repo's ticket states to a Hermes
Kanban board via the dashboard plugin API at /api/plugins/kanban/.

Config (in .todo/config.json):
  hermes: {
    dashboard_url: "http://localhost:<port>",
    board: "<slug>",
    session_token: "<token>" | env: "HERMES_SESSION_TOKEN"
  }
Absent config => `todo sync` errors with setup instructions.

State map (todo → Kanban column):
  open    → todo
  active  → running
  blocked → blocked
  done    → done
  dropped → archived

Sync semantics (push-only, .todo/ is source of truth):
- For each local ticket, GET /api/plugins/kanban/tasks/<id>?board=<slug>.
- If not found: POST /tasks with title=summary, body=description, status
  mapped from local state.
- If found and column differs: PATCH /tasks/<id> with new status.
- Use a stable id mapping (local ticket id == Kanban task id) if Hermes
  accepts user-supplied ids; otherwise store the returned Kanban id in
  ticket.external_refs.hermes_task_id and use it on subsequent syncs.
- Flags: --dry-run (print what would change), --quiet (only errors), 
  --board <override>.

Done contract: integration test that stands up a mock Hermes server
(or vitest fetch mock) and verifies POST/PATCH bodies for each transition.

analysis.author: anonlessness
analysis.content: Spec drift: description used 'dropped → archived' but src/types.ts defines states as open|active|blocked|done|wontfix|duplicate. Implementing the real mapping: open→todo, active→running, blocked→blocked, done→done, wontfix→archived, duplicate→archived.

resolution.note: todo sync pushes state to Hermes Kanban via HTTP plugin API. 15 tests, full suite 156 passing. Spec drift on 'dropped' state corrected via analysis entry to wontfix/duplicate→archived.
